name: AVERROES
fullName: Apixaban in Patients with Atrial Fibrillation Unsuitable for VKA
teachingPoint: Supports anticoagulation over aspirin for AF stroke prevention generally; the ICH-survivor question is answered by the dedicated trials below.

name: ARTESiA
fullName: Apixaban for Stroke Prevention in Subclinical Atrial Fibrillation
population: n=4012, ≥55, NIHSS n/a, subclinical AF detected on device
teachingPoint: Establishes apixaban benefit in subclinical AF; the ICH-survivor question is addressed by PRESTIGE-AF, SoSTART, APACHE-AF and the COCROACH meta-analysis, which enrolled that population.

name: PRESTIGE-AF
fullName: Prevention of Stroke in Intracerebral Haemorrhage Survivors with Atrial Fibrillation
population: n=319, median 79, NIHSS n/a, Spontaneous ICH survivors with AF, mRS ≤4
finding: Ischaemic stroke markedly reduced (0.83 vs 8.60 per 100 patient-years); recurrent ICH did NOT meet non-inferiority (5.00 vs 0.82 per 100 patient-years)
teachingPoint: Objective: anticoagulation after ICH in AF trades a large ischaemic-stroke reduction against a substantial recurrent-ICH increase; individualize.

name: ENRICH-AF
fullName: Edoxaban for Intracranial Hemorrhage Survivors with Atrial Fibrillation
population: n=948, ≥45, NIHSS n/a, Intracranial-haemorrhage survivors with high-risk AF (CHA₂DS₂-VASc ≥2)
finding: Full efficacy not yet reported; the lobar-ICH subgroup was stopped by the DSMB for excess recurrent haemorrhagic stroke
teachingPoint: Objective: interim DSMB stopped edoxaban in lobar ICH for excess rebleeding; the main efficacy comparison is awaited.

name: SoSTART
fullName: Start or Stop Anticoagulants Randomised Trial
population: n=203, adults, NIHSS n/a, Survivors of spontaneous intracranial haemorrhage with AF (CHA₂DS₂-VASc ≥2)
finding: Inconclusive: 8/101 (8%) start vs 4/102 (4%) avoid; starting NOT shown non-inferior
teachingPoint: Objective: did not establish non-inferiority of starting anticoagulation after ICH; hypothesis-generating.

name: APACHE-AF
fullName: Apixaban After Anticoagulation-Associated Intracerebral Haemorrhage in Atrial Fibrillation
population: n=101, median 78, NIHSS n/a, 7-90 d after anticoagulation-associated ICH; AF, CHA₂DS₂-VASc ≥2, mRS ≤4
finding: No difference: 26% (12.6%/y) apixaban vs 24% (11.9%/y) avoid; high absolute risk in both arms
teachingPoint: Objective: no clear difference; both strategies carry high residual vascular risk.

name: COCROACH
fullName: Oral Anticoagulation after Intracranial Haemorrhage in AF: Individual-Patient-Data Meta-analysis
population: n=412, 75% ≥75, NIHSS n/a, AF with prior spontaneous intracranial haemorrhage (pooled RCTs: SoSTART, APACHE-AF, NASPAF-ICH)
teachingPoint: Objective synthesis: net benefit of anticoagulation after ICH in AF remains statistically uncertain; ischaemic reduction is offset by a non-significant bleeding increase.
